topic: A person correctly using a French Press to brew coffee, countertop view.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Front view: hands scoop coffee grounds into the French press.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Top-down: the person pours hot water over the grounds and stirs briefly.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Side-angle: the person presses the plunger and the brewed coffee pours into a mug on the countertop.
parsed_generation.narration: On the countertop, a person uses a French press. The sequence shows grounds being added, hot water poured and stirred, then the plunger pressed and coffee poured into a mug.

feedback: Overall aligns with the topic by showing a countertop French press sequence with three distinct shots. It lacks the extended, texture-rich pacing of the gold standard (bloom/wait, steam, and a more deliberate pour). To improve, add a bloom/wait moment, incorporate a close-up of steam or coffee texture, and align cadence with explicit timestamped beats (e.g., [00:04-00:07]) to mirror the gold standard’s pacing.
strengths: Clear three-shot sequence focused on the French press
Uses varied camera angles (front, top-down, side-angle) for cinematic feel
fixes: Increase visual richness with steam, coffee texture, and cup/material details
Introduce a longer, paced sequence (bloom/wait) and a final pour close-up to match the gold-standard cadence
